title: Day 1 — Lock your Niche & Service
objective: Eliminate distraction by committing to one specific audience and one core mechanism.
tasks: Select ONE target niche (e.g., HVAC companies, e-commerce brands under $10k/mo, med spas).
Select ONE service mechanism you will deliver (e.g., short-form TikTok editing, Meta ads, cold email setup).
Write your commitment down in the Founder OS profile and do not change it for 30 days.
success_criteria: You can state exactly who you help and what you do in one sentence without hesitating.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: The user selected niche: {{niche}} and service: {{service}}. Act as a strict business coach. Evaluate if this combination is profitable and specific enough. If it's too broad (like 'fitness'), ruthlessly tell them to narrow down (e.g., 'crossfit gyms in the UK').

title: Day 2 — Craft Your Grand Slam Offer
objective: Transform your generic service into a tangible, measurable result.
tasks: Define the specific dream outcome your service delivers (e.g., '10 extra booked appointments').
Define the timeline for the outcome (e.g., 'within 30 days').
Draft a risk-reversal guarantee (e.g., 'or you don't pay').
success_criteria: You have a single, formulated offer statement written in the format: 'I help [niche] achieve [outcome] through [service] in [timeframe].'
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's niche is {{niche}}, service is {{service}}. They drafted this offer: {{user_input}}. Rewrite this offer to make it punchier, more specific, and irresistible according to Alex Hormozi's $100M Offers framework.

title: Day 3 — Baseline Pricing Strategy
objective: Establish a rigid pricing floor so you don't race to the bottom.
tasks: Research 3 competitors to find the standard market rate for your service.
Set your minimum acceptable price for the next 30 days (do not set this lower than $500/mo or $500 flat).
Write down the exact phrase you will use to present your price on a call.
success_criteria: You have a concrete dollar amount pricing floor established in your dashboard.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User wants to charge ${{price}} for {{service}} to {{niche}}. Act as a strict sales coach. Tell them if they are undercharging, and give them a script on how to confidently state a higher price on a sales call without sounding nervous.

title: Day 4 — Optimize the Foundation
objective: Ensure your primary outbound profile (Twitter, LinkedIn, or IG) looks like a business, not a personal diary.
tasks: Change your profile bio to your exact Offer Statement from Day 2.
Update your profile picture to a clean, well-lit headshot.
Remove or archive any unprofessional personal posts that do not establish authority.
success_criteria: If a prospect clicks your profile from a cold DM, they instantly know what you sell within 3 seconds.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is optimizing their profile on {{platform}} for {{niche}}. Give them 3 strict, non-negotiable rules for how their profile must look to convert {{niche}} clients, specific to {{platform}}.

title: Day 5 — Build Your Lead Pipeline
objective: Construct the tracking mechanism you will use every day to avoid losing prospects.
tasks: Open the Founder OS Kanban Board (or a simple Google Sheet if unavailable).
Create columns: Lead, Contacted, Replied, Call Booked, Closed.
Familiarize yourself with logging a test lead and moving it across the board.
success_criteria: You have a functioning, empty pipeline ready to accept raw data.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User asserts they don't need a CRM and can 'just remember' who they DM'd. Berate this amateur mindset. Briefly explain why pipeline tracking is the only difference between hobbyists and professionals.

title: Day 6 — Source the First 50 Targets
objective: Gather a highly qualified list of businesses so you aren't hunting for leads *while* doing outreach.
tasks: Identify the best platform to find your niche (e.g., Apollo, Instagram hashtags, Google Maps).
Scrape or manually find 50 specific companies/founders that fit your exact criteria.
Log their Name, Company, and Profile Link/Email into your Lead Pipeline.
success_criteria: You have exactly 50 qualified target names locked into your pipeline board in the 'Lead' column.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's niche is {{niche}}. Give them exactly 3 specific, tactical methods to scrape or find 50 high-quality leads for this exact niche online, step-by-step.

title: Day 7 — Draft the Cold Outreach Script
objective: Finalize a low-friction script designed entirely to get a reply, not immediately close a deal.
tasks: Write a one-sentence personalized hook based on the prospect's recent activity.
Write a one-sentence pitch utilizing your Offer Statement.
Write a low-friction Call to Action (e.g., 'Mind if I send over a quick video breakdown?').
success_criteria: You have a base, 3-sentence outreach script ready to copy-paste and slightly modify.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User wrote this outreach script: {{user_input}} for niche: {{niche}}. Tear it down. If it's too long, cut it. If it sounds 'salesy', rewrite it to be casual. Provide them with a finalized, 3-sentence high-converting template.

title: Day 8 — Initiate First Contact
objective: Break the ice and transition from planning to execution.
tasks: Select the first 20 leads from your pipeline.
Customize the hook of your script for each of the 20 leads.
Send all 20 messages and move them to 'Contacted' in your pipeline.
success_criteria: 20 messages are physically sent and tracked.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is scared to hit send on their first 20 messages. Give them a harsh reality check about volume and rejection. Tell them why their feelings do not matter to the math of business.

title: Day 9 — Feed the Machine
objective: Build the daily habit of sourcing and sending.
tasks: Source 20 brand new leads to replace the ones you contacted yesterday.
Add them to the pipeline.
Send 20 personalized outreaches to the next batch in your pipeline.
success_criteria: You maintained 50 untouched leads in the pipeline and sent an additional 20 outreaches.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's niche is {{niche}}. They are complaining that sourcing 20 leads took too long. Give them a workflow optimization tip (a specific tool or search parameter) to cut lead sourcing time in half.

title: Day 10 — Volume & First Follow-ups
objective: Learn that the money is in the follow-up.
tasks: Send 20 new outreaches to fresh leads.
Find the 20 leads from Day 8 who did not respond.
Send a 1-sentence bump message (e.g., 'Just floating this to the top of your inbox').
success_criteria: 20 new DMs sent + 20 follow-ups executed.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is afraid to follow up because they feel 'annoying'. Correct this weak mindset. Explain that business owners are busy, not ignoring them, and persistence is a requirement for respect.

title: Day 11 — The 3-Day Review
objective: Look at the data and adjust if the script is failing.
tasks: Calculate your Open/Reply rate from the 60 messages sent so far.
If you have 0 replies, tweak the angle of your initial hook based on the Founder OS templates.
Send 20 new outreaches using the (potentially adjusted) script.
success_criteria: You tracked your metrics in the dashboard and completed 20 outreaches.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User sent 60 messages with script: {{user_input}} and got {{replies}} replies. If replies are 0, formulate a radically different angle. If replies are >0, tell them not to change anything and just keep sending.

title: Day 12 — Consistent Execution
objective: Prove you can execute when the initial excitement fades.
tasks: Source 20 new leads.
Follow up with the 20 leads from Day 9.
Send 20 new outreaches.
success_criteria: All pipeline columns updated, 20 initial DMs and 20 follow-ups sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User feels bored and unmotivated today. Act as a strict coach. Tell them why motivation is a myth and discipline is the only thing separating them from the founders they watch on YouTube.

title: Day 13 — Objection Defense Setup
objective: Prepare to handle replies without panicking or dropping leads.
tasks: Write down the 3 most likely objections your niche will give (e.g., 'We do this in-house', 'No budget').
Draft a 1-2 sentence rebuttal for each designed to pivot to a phone call.
Send 20 new outreaches.
success_criteria: You have an objection-handling cheat sheet ready next to your pipeline, and 20 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User expects objections from {{niche}} regarding {{service}}. Provide a high-converting rebuttal script for 'We don't have budget right now' and pivot it to booking a 10-minute discovery call.

title: Day 14 — End of Week 2 Review
objective: Audit your first full week of volume.
tasks: Log your total Week 2 metrics in the metrics dashboard (DMs sent, Replies, Bookings).
Follow up with ANY lead from this week that hasn't responded to the 2nd message.
Send 20 new outreaches.
success_criteria: A completely updated dashboard, zero leads dropped, 20 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User just logged their Week 2 metrics. Give them a brutally honest assessment of their output based on standard SaaS B2B conversion rates. Push them to increase volume next week.

title: Day 15 — Scale Up the Volume
objective: Now that you possess the habit, increase the output by 50%.
tasks: Source 40 new leads.
Send 30 new outreaches.
Respond to any active replies within 1 hour.
success_criteria: 30 new outreaches sent and pipeline fully updated.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User claims they 'don't have time' to send 30 DMs a day. Break down the math. Show them that at 3 minutes per DM, it is only 90 minutes of work. Demand they find the time.

title: Day 16 — Sales Call Preparation
objective: Prepare the framework for your first Zoom discovery call.
tasks: Write down a 5-step call framework (Intro, Discovery/Questions, Pitch, Objections, Close).
Draft 3 deep discovery questions specifically tailored to your niche's pain points.
Send 30 new outreaches.
success_criteria: You have a physical document/script to look at while conducting your eventual sales call, and 30 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User needs discovery questions for {{niche}} around {{service}}. Generate 3 high-impact, emotionally compelling questions that expose the prospect's pain point and need for the user's service.

title: Day 17 — Volume & Velocity
objective: Maintain the new output level.
tasks: Send 30 new outreaches.
Execute follow-ups on Day 15's unresponded leads.
Update pipeline.
success_criteria: 30 outreaches + required follow-ups executed.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is starting to hit the 'trough of sorrow' where excitement fades and results might still be low. Give them a harsh reminder that winners execute regardless of their emotional state.

title: Day 18 — Mock Call Rehearsal
objective: Remove the initial panic of speaking to a prospect live.
tasks: Record yourself reading your pitch and handling an objection for 5 minutes.
Review the recording and fix your cadence (slow down, eliminate filler words).
Send 30 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Audio/video recorded securely, pitch adjusted, 30 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User recorded a mock call and feels awkward. Explain the concept of 'reps' to them. Provide 2 specific techniques to project authority on a Zoom call even if they are young or inexperienced.

title: Day 19 — Replenish the Funnel
objective: Never let the pipeline dry up.
tasks: Source 50 new qualified leads to pad out the rest of the week.
Send 30 new outreaches.
Follow up with all leads who responded previously but went ghost.
success_criteria: Pipeline loaded with 50 leads, 30 DMs out the door.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's niche is {{niche}}. Ask them to verify their lead quality. Are they messaging broke businesses? Explain how to identify high-ticket clients vs low-budget headaches just by looking at their profile/website.

title: Day 20 — Follow-Up Friday
objective: Capitalize on people clearing out their inboxes before the weekend.
tasks: Send a 3rd follow-up attempt to leads from Week 2 who never replied ('Going to cross you off my list...').
Send a 'Any thoughts on this?' bump to recent non-responders.
Send 30 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Aggressive follow-up sweep completed across the entire pipeline. 30 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User doesn't want to send a 'breakup message' follow-up because it feels rude. Explain the psychology of the 'takeaway' close and give them a highly professional breakup template.

title: Day 21 — End of Week 3 Audit
objective: Assess if the scale-up to 30/day improved outcomes proportionally.
tasks: Log all Week 3 metrics (Sent, Replies, Bookings).
Identify the #1 bottleneck (e.g., getting replies BUT they don't want to book a call).
Send 30 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Metrics logged, bottleneck identified, 30 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's bottleneck is {{user_input}} (e.g., getting replies but no bookings). Act as a strict sales strategist and give them an exact pivot mechanism to fix this specific drop-off point.

title: Day 22 — Push to Absolute Maximum
objective: Hit the daily volume peak required to guarantee luck.
tasks: Source 50 new leads.
Send 40 new outreaches.
Execute all follow-ups.
success_criteria: You hit the ceiling: 40 cold outreaches sent in one day.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User feels exhausted at the thought of 40 DMs. Reframe their perspective. Tell them about founders who send 150 DMs manually. Remind them why they started.

title: Day 23 — The Feedback Loop
objective: Analyze the objections encountered so far and structurally solve them.
tasks: Find the most common objection you've received in replies or calls.
Modify your Offer Statement or Guarantee to preemptively destroy that objection.
Send 40 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Offer updated based on real market feedback, 40 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User's most common objection is {{user_input}}. Tell them exactly how to adjust their actual service delivery or guarantee to make this objection completely irrelevant.

title: Day 24 — Closing Technique Mastery
objective: Prepare to ask for the money without flinching.
tasks: Write down your exact statement to close (e.g., 'So, based on what we've covered, do you want to move forward for $X?').
Commit to remaining absolutely silent after asking that question until the prospect speaks.
Send 40 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Close statement written, silence rule absorbed, 40 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is afraid of the awkward silence after stating their price. Explain the psychology of silence in sales. Strict instruction: Whoever speaks first, loses.

title: Day 25 — Total Pipeline Saturation
objective: Maximizing outbound volume.
tasks: Source 50 new leads.
Send 40 new outreaches.
Ensure not a single pipeline lead is 'stuck' without a next defined action.
success_criteria: Deep clean of the pipeline completed, 40 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User complains about pipeline management being boring. Tell them CRM hygiene is the hallmark of a CEO, while disorganized DMs are the hallmark of a freelancer who stays broke.

title: Day 26 — The Unbelievable Follow-Up
objective: Use creative methods for cold leads.
tasks: Find 5 highly qualified leads who never replied.
Record a custom 60-second Loom video for each auditing their business.
Send the video links and send 35 standard textual outreaches.
success_criteria: 5 Loom videos deployed, 35 text DMs sent. 40 total.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User doesn't know what to say in a Loom video for {{niche}}. Give them a 3-part framework: Hook (Why I'm making this), Value (One thing they are doing wrong I found), CTA (Let's fix it on a call).

title: Day 27 — Volume Commitment
objective: Treating the volume as a non-negotiable heartbeat of the business.
tasks: Send 40 new outreaches.
Run your Follow-Up Friday sweep.
Update pipeline metrics.
success_criteria: 40 outreaches sent, follow-up sweep processed.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: Coach the user on the compound effect. Explain that the 40 DMs sent today will likely not pay off for 2-3 weeks, and why they must trust the invisible momentum.

title: Day 28 — Pre-Launch Systematization
objective: Document your workflow so you can stop thinking and just execute.
tasks: Write out your exact daily schedule for Day 31+ (e.g., 9AM Source, 10AM Send, 11AM Follow up).
Send 40 new outreaches.
success_criteria: Schedule rigidly defined on paper, 40 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User wants to 'take a break' after day 30. Destroy this concept. Tell them Day 30 is the starting line, not the finish line.

title: Day 29 — Final Push & Fulfillment Prep
objective: Ensure you are ready to actually deliver the service when they pay.
tasks: Draft a 1-page onboarding document or form to send to your first closed client.
Send 40 new outreaches.
Follow up with all active conversations.
success_criteria: Onboarding doc created, 40 DMs sent.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User is worried they won't know how to deliver the service once a client pays. Reassure them with the 'sell first, build later' ethos. Strict instruction to focus on revenue, not hypothetical delivery issues.

title: Day 30 — The Graduate Review
objective: Analyze the entirety of the 30-day sprint and graduate to operational status.
tasks: Log your final 30-day aggregate metrics (Total Sent, Total Replies, Total Books, Total Revenue).
Acknowledge your success in completing the 30 days regardless of revenue outcome.
Send 40 new outreaches (The work doesn't stop).
success_criteria: Final metrics captured in Founder OS dashboard and 40 DMs sent. Graduation achieved.
ai_coaching_prompt_template: User just finished Day 30. Look at their metrics: {{metrics}}. If revenue > 0, congratulate them highly but demand they scale. If revenue is 0, give an analytical breakdown that volume must continue for 60 more days to see the lagging ROI.
